rubric_results.check: Title is non-empty and plausibly the document's title?
rubric_results.note: The title references a specific reservation land addition and an EIS, which is a plausible government document title.

rubric_results.check: Length is reasonable (not a sentence fragment, not the whole abstract)?
rubric_results.note: Title is concise and appropriately scoped for a document title.

rubric_results.check: If two sources (NUL + Haiku) — do they roughly agree?
rubric_results.note: Only one source (NUL) was provided; no Haiku source to compare.

title.notes: The extracted title is plausible and well-formed for a government EIS document concerning the Havasupai Indian Reservation. However, no cited page text was provided to verify the extraction, and only a single source (NUL) was used. Confidence is medium due to the absence of corroborating evidence from cited pages or a second source.

rubric_results.check: Year falls within 1969 ≤ year ≤ current year?
rubric_results.note: 2026 is a future year beyond the current year (2025), failing the upper bound check

rubric_results.check: Year present in NUL metadata OR found on first 3 pages?
rubric_results.note: NUL metadata reports 2026; however, no cited page text was provided to verify regex finding of 1979

rubric_results.check: If NUL and regex disagree, value reflects this with a low-confidence flag?
rubric_results.note: Extraction correctly flags disagreement between NUL=2026 and regex=1979 with low confidence

year.notes: There is a direct conflict between NUL metadata (2026, a future year outside valid range) and regex extraction from the first 3 pages (1979, a plausible EIS year). No cited page text was provided to adjudicate the disagreement. The regex value of 1979 is far more plausible for an EIS document, but cannot be confirmed without source text; human review is required.

rubric_results.check: Type is one of allowed values
rubric_results.note: 'Draft' is a valid type in the allowed set

rubric_results.check: Regex hit on first page and Sonnet on first 2 pages agree
rubric_results.note: Both sources listed as 'regex (first page)' and 'Sonnet (first 2 pages)' agree on 'Draft'

rubric_results.check: Evidence phrase supports the chosen type
rubric_results.note: 'DRAFT ENVIRONMENTAL STATEMENT / (X) Draft' directly and unambiguously supports the 'Draft' classification

eis_type.notes: The extracted value 'Draft' is strongly supported by the evidence phrase, which contains both a header label 'DRAFT ENVIRONMENTAL STATEMENT' and a checkbox indicator '(X) Draft'. Both extraction methods agree. The only caveat is that no cited page text was provided for independent verification, so the evidence relies solely on the extracted metadata rather than raw document text.

rubric_results.check: All listed agencies appear in NUL contributors OR on cited title pages?
rubric_results.note: United States. Bureau of Indian Affairs is sourced from NUL and confirmed present there.

rubric_results.check: Joint-lead (>2 agencies) flagged for human review?
rubric_results.note: Only one agency listed; joint-lead check not applicable.

rubric_results.check: No role text left in agency names?
rubric_results.note: The agency name contains no parenthetical role text or descriptors such as '(issuing body)'.

lead_agency.notes: A single lead agency, United States. Bureau of Indian Affairs, is extracted cleanly with no role text and is confirmed via the NUL source. No cited title pages were provided, but the NUL attribution is sufficient for verification. No joint-lead concern applies here.

rubric_results.check: project_description text supported by cited pages
rubric_results.note: PL 93-620 adding ~185,000 acres, 20 plan divisions, four residential areas (Pasture Wash, south of Hualapai Hilltop, Moqui Tank, east of Topocoba Hilltop), tribal cattle herd, road improvements, rustic campsites, prohibitions on commercial timber/mining/industrial development, and 'remain forever wild' language all appear explicitly in the cited pages.

rubric_results.check: Numbers, places, and dates match cited pages
rubric_results.note: 185,000 acres, 95,300 acres traditional use area, four named residential areas, 20 plan divisions, January 3 1975 signing date, 1985 Raintank transfer date, and 'forever wild' language all confirmed in the cited text.

rubric_results.check: affected_community figures (425 members, ~60 families, 281-316 population, 71% federal income) supported by cited pages
rubric_results.note: The cited pages provided do not include the Social and Economic section (pages II-40 onward with specific tribal population and income statistics). The text references ~60 families in the Residence section (I-9), but the specific figures of 425 enrolled members, 281-316 reservation population in 1976-1977, and 71% federal income are not visible in the extracted page content provided for review.

rubric_results.check: alternatives_overview supported by cited pages
rubric_results.note: No Action alternative, domestic water alternatives (atmospheric catchment, wells, rim aquifers, piping from Havasu Creek, treating stock-tank water), energy options (generators, commercial power line, wind/solar), and road realignment options including Topocoba Road relocation are all explicitly described in the cited pages.

rubric_results.check: environmental_impact details (1,200+ archaeological sites, bighorn sheep, Cohonina sites, specific vegetation types) supported by cited pages
rubric_results.note: The cited pages provided do not include the Environmental Impact chapter (Chapter III) or the Cultural Resources section with the 1,200+ recorded sites figure. The table of contents references these sections but the actual content is not in the extracted text provided.

rubric_results.check: public_response based_on_main_doc_only=true and limited to main doc
rubric_results.note: The public_response field is marked based_on_main_doc_only=true and the content references the EIS document itself, PL 93-620 requirements, and tribal positions described within the document.

rubric_results.check: public_response content (federal agencies list, 90-day congressional transmittal, tribal positions) supported by cited pages
rubric_results.note: The list of federal agencies, state entities, and tribes from whom comments were requested appears on the cover pages. The 90-day congressional transmittal requirement is explicitly stated in PL 93-620 text on page I-3/I-4. Tribal positions on preserving near-natural state, restricting aircraft, and Topocoba road relocation are in the cited pages.

rubric_results.check: No invention or hallucinated detail beyond cited content
rubric_results.note: Specific statistics in affected_community (425 enrolled members, 281-316 population, 71% federal income) and environmental_impact (1,200+ recorded archaeological sites, specific Cohonina/sweat lodge/rockshelter details) are not visible in the provided cited page extracts, raising concern about whether these are supported or hallucinated.

summary.notes: The project_description, alternatives_overview, and public_response fields are well-supported by the visible cited page content. However, the affected_community field contains specific quantitative claims (425 enrolled members, 281-316 population, 71% federal income) and the environmental_impact field contains specific details (1,200+ archaeological sites, Cohonina sites, sweat lodges) that are not present in the extracted page text provided for review—these likely appear in sections of the document not included in the cited page extraction. The extraction may be accurate if those pages were reviewed by the extractor but are simply not shown here; human review is needed to confirm whether those figures appear in the source document's social/economic and cultural resources sections.

rubric_results.check: Each listed alternative is actually discussed on its cited page?
rubric_results.note: No alternatives were extracted and no cited pages were provided, so this check cannot be performed.

rubric_results.check: The No Action alternative is included if the cited text includes it?
rubric_results.note: No cited text was provided, so it is impossible to verify whether a No Action alternative should have been included.

rubric_results.check: Names and descriptions match terminology used in the cited text?
rubric_results.note: No extracted names or descriptions exist to compare against any source text.

rubric_results.check: No outside-knowledge alternatives added that aren't in the chapter?
rubric_results.note: The extracted value is an empty list, so no outside-knowledge alternatives were introduced.

alternatives.notes: The extraction returned an empty result with no cited pages, citing a structural detection failure and a deliberate skip per the v2 plan. Because no alternatives content was captured at all, the extraction is incomplete and must be re-run. The pipeline should apply a fallback strategy (e.g., keyword search for 'alternatives', 'No Action', 'proposed action') to locate and extract the relevant chapter before this field can be validated.

rubric_results.check: Each chosen theme is supported by the doc summary fields?
rubric_results.note: No cited pages were provided, so the summary text cannot be verified. The justification references specific content (Havasupai Tribal Council, Public Law 93-620, bighorn sheep, multi-agency review) but there is no source text to confirm these claims against.

rubric_results.check: Theme names come from the fixed taxonomy verbatim?
rubric_results.note: No taxonomy was provided to verify against. Cannot confirm 'Indigenous Narratives and Sovereignty', 'Wildlife and Natural Areas', and 'Governance and Institutional Control' are verbatim taxonomy entries.

rubric_results.check: Subthemes belong to the chosen theme(s) per the taxonomy?
rubric_results.note: Without the fixed taxonomy, subtheme-to-theme mapping cannot be verified. Assignments appear plausible on face value but cannot be confirmed.

rubric_results.check: 1–3 themes and 2–5 subthemes selected (within range)?
rubric_results.note: 3 themes and 5 subthemes selected, both within the allowed ranges.

themes.notes: No cited page text was provided, making it impossible to verify that the extracted themes and subthemes are grounded in the actual document summary. Additionally, the fixed taxonomy was not supplied, so verbatim name matching and subtheme-to-theme assignments cannot be confirmed. A human reviewer should validate the source content and cross-check against the taxonomy before accepting this extraction.

rubric_results.check: Place names appear in cited pages?
rubric_results.note: Havasupai Indian Reservation Addition, Grand Canyon National Park, northwestern/northern Arizona all appear explicitly in the cited pages.

rubric_results.check: is_multi_site set correctly?
rubric_results.note: The project covers a single 185,000-acre reservation addition; is_multi_site=false is correct.

rubric_results.check: For corridor projects: endpoints named, not just a midpoint?
rubric_results.note: This is not a corridor project; it is a discrete land parcel.

rubric_results.check: State abbreviations match U.S. conventions?
rubric_results.note: AZ is the correct USPS abbreviation for Arizona.

location.notes: All place names in the extracted value are confirmed in the cited pages. The Havasupai Indian Reservation Addition in Arizona (AZ) is clearly and repeatedly referenced throughout the document. The single-site designation and state abbreviation are both correct.

rubric_results.check: Agency preparer named in cited Preparers/Consultation text?
rubric_results.note: The cited text explicitly states 'The University of Arizona (Office of Arid Lands Studies) was invited during February, 1977 to submit a proposal to prepare an environmental impact statement' and lists 'University of Arizona / Office of Arid Lands Studies' under consultation entities.

rubric_results.check: Cooperating agencies/tribal nations actually listed in cited text?
rubric_results.note: All listed cooperating agencies appear in the consultation list on page IX-1/IX-2: BIA Truxton Canyon Agency, BIA Phoenix Area Office, U.S. Forest Service Tusayan District, NPS Grand Canyon National Park, Western Archaeological Center, Arizona State Museum, Arizona State University, Arizona Fish and Game, Museum of Northern Arizona, State Historic Preservation Officer, and The Havasupai Tribe of Arizona. However, 'Arizona State Historic Preservation Officer' is listed in the text as 'State Historic Preservation Officer' — minor naming discrepancy but clearly the same entity.

rubric_results.check: For public_commenters: stance present on cited page? Quote verbatim? Speaker clear?
rubric_results.note: No public commenters were extracted; field is empty.

rubric_results.check: Private individuals: last-name-only or 'private commenter' convention followed?
rubric_results.note: No private individuals were extracted as public commenters. Named individuals in the consultation list (e.g., Mr. Harley Shaw, Mr. Tom Britt) were correctly omitted from the extraction.

key_people.notes: The extraction accurately captures the EIS preparer (Office of Arid Lands Studies, University of Arizona) and all cooperating agencies/tribal nations listed in the Chapter IX consultation text. The only minor discrepancy is 'Arizona State Historic Preservation Officer' vs. the text's 'State Historic Preservation Officer,' which is immaterial. No public commenters were extracted, which is appropriate as the cited pages contain no public comment section. The source page range '157-292' is unusually broad but the relevant consultation content is present in the cited text.
